content.body: Nice visualization! That does indeed seem to strongly argue for making source a twin of relatedTarget and making the relevant dispatch calls that have a source also make the event composed.
As long as it's not a deal breaker for folks to have the event bubble to the shadow roots in between
Jake Archibald might have more of an idea there, as would dandclark (who is presumably not awake right now)
content.formatted_body: Jake Archibald might have more of an idea there, as would dandclark (who is presumably not awake right now)

content.body: It was honestly quite a surprise to me that `relatedTarget` has the effect of seemingly overriding `composed` once you reach the common ancestor root
content.formatted_body: It was honestly quite a surprise to me that relatedTarget has the effect of seemingly overriding composed once you reach the common ancestor root

content.body: now, with  submit, can submitter be null? Since if yes, then I guess in such case event shouldn't be composed. So maybe some of these events should  have .composed = source || submitter || <foo>;
submitter sets source, right?
https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/10995/changes#diff-41cf6794ba4200b839c53531555f0f3998df4cbb01a4d5cb0b94e3ca5e23947dR64995
Right, but I mean [this](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#:~:text=Let%20submitterButton%20be%20null%20if%20submitter%20is%20form%2E).
And since dom spec uses relatedTarget as a concept, not attribute value, we could just say that source/submitter/etc are the relatedTarget concept, just the same way .relatedTarget is in some events
what about it, sorry?
i.e. replace the proposed "source" concept with just reusing the "related target" concept?

I meant just that submitter can be null. And since it can be null and we want to avoid breaking sites too badly, we probably want to keep submit event not-composed when submitter is null so that the event doesn't start to propagate to light dom unexpectedly
right. Would that work?

I'm confused on a couple of things:
- the thing you linked to sets `submitterButton` to null based on the value of `submitter` - is `submitterButton` the thing we actually want to check?
- why do we need to check `submitter` directly at all when it's already used as the `source` (or, if we go with your suggestion, `relatedTarget` value)?
content.formatted_body:
I'm confused on a couple of things:

  • the thing you linked to sets submitterButton to null based on the value of submitter - is submitterButton the thing we actually want to check?
  • why do we need to check submitter directly at all when it's already used as the source (or, if we go with your suggestion, relatedTarget value)?

content.body: smaug: jarhar: I think for `option` elements in theory we could use the `content` property, but nobody supports that on elements with non-image values?
content.formatted_body: smaug: jarhar: I think for option elements in theory we could use the content property, but nobody supports that on elements with non-image values?

content.body: This would be a useful feature for other appearance base things (e.g. ::file-selector-button) but it's probably a big lift?
We had an implementation of that and it was not web compatible
But maybe for options we could make it work, or so
But tbf for options it would only be UA styles that needed it right? That should be more compatible
* But tbf for options it would only be UA styles that needed it right? That should be more compatible. Not saying it's the right thing to do, shadow dom seems probably fine?

m.new_content.body: But tbf for options it would only be UA styles that needed it right? That should be more compatible. Not saying it's the right thing to do, shadow dom seems probably fine?

content.body: FYI for random readers, the proposal got documented here https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/10995#issuecomment-4572681552
smaug: https://github.com/whatwg/dom/pull/1469 BoundaryPointsRange mixin; this was easier than expected
